title: Strategic Steps for Higher Education Leadership to Train Teams Against Algorithmic Bias
author: Fatile, Mopelola
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
This book, “Strategic Steps for Higher Education Leadership to Train Teams Against Algorithmic Bias,” provides a detailed guide for higher education leaders who want to ensure that algorithms and AI systems used in universities and colleges are fair, transparent, and ethically responsible. Also, it provides a structured approach, based on ethics and institutional values, to help leaders incorporate accountability for algorithmic bias into various aspects of higher education governance and practice.  
  It begins with a thorough review—called an Institutional Audit—of the algorithms and tools currently in use, helping leaders identify risks and biases. From there, it guides the development of decision-making protocols that emphasize transparency and fairness. The book also emphasizes the importance of faculty development to build understanding and literacy around algorithms, and advocates for inclusive policies around data governance to involve diverse stakeholders in governing data ethically.  
  Further, the book discusses how to embed ethical standards into student success programs and how to integrate AI accountability into long-term strategic planning. The final chapter encourages academic leaders to foster a culture of responsibility and ethics, challenging inherited biases and enhancing institutional commitment in shaping AI use.  
  Targeting provosts, deans, faculty leaders, and doctoral scholars, the book advocates for a leadership approach that is proactive, inclusive, and precise. It emphasizes that maintaining algorithmic integrity should become a fundamental part of how institutions govern themselves and strive for excellence, ensuring that AI systems serve the core values of higher education.
